cb65425b-cc3d-4f8a-8164-5849f594d708.title: Build data by onboarding 5 additional customers
cb65425b-cc3d-4f8a-8164-5849f594d708.exec_summary: Goal: onboard the next 5 hand-picked customers and run AP & AR reconciliation on neno by early July, with onboarding calls targeted for 6-7 July (Joel building next week while Matthew is away). Open Banking ingestion is the active build (Dima); the provider is selected (Montoya) and the contract has now been received (24 Jun), with prod-key gating requirements being scoped this week before the first full preview test - As Controla (non-Swan) is the first target customer. Per-client Exact connections progressed: per-workspace Exact writes + GL/VAT reads (M2) shipped, and a follow-up to separate OAuth grants from workspace division assignments is in progress after the team confirmed Exact tokens are per-connection (a new per-connection token table is needed before writes are fully usable). The redesigned onboarding dashboard shipped, and a new policy now requires vibe-coded work to ship in small reviewable batches or stay frontend-only. The guided onboarding home and the WhatsApp/email/Vault channels are queued next, then final QA. Main worries: the 6-7 Jul target is tight (onboarding-actions still planned, its stepper depends on a new transactions UI), Swan blockers (public onboarding link + Onboarding V2 migration) need fixing by 26 Jun, the WhatsApp/Meta Business setup is currently misconfigured, and the Exact 60 req/min rate limit is already being hit on seeded data.
whats_changed: Onboarding-actions design firmed up across two 25 Jun sessions (Onboarding Actions with Dima; Three amigos): magic-link sign-on, a self-marked actions checklist reusing existing pages, and Dima + Eugenia owning the build while Matthew is away.

Homepage UX decided: lighter layout (away from chunky cards), active task highlighted in purple, white cards; completed tasks hide and the homepage is removed from nav once done (invoice task optional); the accountant profile block is copied to the tasks screen so it survives the homepage being hidden.

Page-visibility rules for open-banking customers: show Account, Transactions and Invoices; Transactions appears once a bank is connected and transactions exist; Bills & expenses is .build-only and out of scope. Ihor & Joel to do the permissions work.
Bank-connect uses the Yapily ('Yapi') one-button hand-off model; homepage shows connected accounts and prompts for more; WhatsApp setup collapses once connected.

Dummy onboarding task: marked as a dummy, types 'clarification' or 're-upload receipt', surfaces WhatsApp + email-forwarding, discoverable from the notification bell and transactions view; all three receipt channels must be explored before it is marked done.
Open-banking provider contract (Montoya) received 24 Jun; prod-key gating being scoped; onboarding calls targeted for 6-7 July.

Multi-Client Exact: per-workspace writes + GL/VAT reads shipped; OAuth-grant rework in progress (tokens are per-connection, not per-division); Exact export automation now runs as a local script with a nightly-sync + GL-migration path designed.

risks.text: 6-7 Jul onboarding target is tight - the onboarding-actions project is still planned and its stepper depends on the new transactions UI, whose drawer still needs significant rework.
Swan blockers for higher card limits + direct debit: public onboarding link must be fixed and migration to Onboarding V2 completed; Nick & Matthew targeting fixes by Fri 26 Jun.
Montoya open-banking contract received 24 Jun; prod keys still gate the open-banking end-to-end test (requirements being scoped).
Exact OAuth rework: a new table to store access/refresh tokens per connection is required before Exact write functionality is fully usable.
Exact API rate limit (60 req/min) already hit in testing with seeded data; needs a roadmap solution (connection pool or backpressure).
WhatsApp/Meta Business account setup is misconfigured and was deprioritised (24 Jun); blocks the WhatsApp bill channel rollout.
Email forwarding only accepts workspace-member senders, has no UI yet, and the rejected-email auto-reply is still to be built.

projects.name: Connect external bank accounts (Open Banking)
projects.summary: Provider-agnostic transaction/balance ingestion plus a multi-account Money Management UI rework.
blockers: Montoya contract unsigned as of 23 Jun; prod keys expected 24 Jun

projects.name: Multi-Client Exact Online Connections
projects.summary: A separate Exact connection per client workspace so accountants reconcile each client into their own division.
blockers: GL/VAT picker error states, dead-connection detection and Ops alerting still outstanding

projects.name: Present onboarding actions for the next 5 customers
projects.summary: Home dashboard with guided steps teaching the three bill-submission channels and routing to bank connect.
blockers: Depends on the new transactions UI

projects.name: WhatsApp, Email forwarding & Vault re-provisioning
projects.summary: Make the three bill-upload channels work for customers without a Swan account.

decisions.text: Onboarding uses magic-link sign-on; customers create a workspace, log in and self-onboard via a homepage actions checklist; reuse existing pages and link out rather than build custom components (separate checklist items, not a modal). Dima + Eugenia own the build.
decisions.source: Granola - Onboarding Actions with Dima, 25 Jun

decisions.text: Onboarding tasks are self-marked; hard-code one task per workspace linking back to the homepage; completed tasks are hidden (not deleted); once all are done the homepage is hidden and removed from nav (invoice task optional). The accountant profile block is copied to the tasks screen so it persists after the homepage is hidden.
decisions.source: Granola - Three amigos / with Dima, 25 Jun

decisions.text: Page visibility for open-banking customers: show Account, Transactions and Invoices; Transactions appears once a bank is connected and transactions exist in the table; Bills & expenses is .build-only and out of scope. Accountant chat exists but is not yet available and is not surfaced in onboarding.
decisions.source: Granola - Onboarding Actions with Dima, 25 Jun

decisions.text: Bank-connect uses the Yapily ('Yapi') model - one button from Neno hands off to the provider-controlled flow; the homepage shows connected accounts and prompts for additional ones; WhatsApp setup collapses once connected.

Dummy onboarding task: marked as a dummy; types 'clarification' or 're-upload receipt'; surfaces the WhatsApp + email-forwarding channels and is discoverable from the notification bell and the transactions view; all three receipt channels must be explored before it is marked done.
Redesigned onboarding dashboard (Giuseppe) shipped to production; new policy: vibe-coded work ships in small reviewable batches or stays frontend-only until engineering assesses feasibility.

Exact OAuth tokens are per-connection, not per-division; one token reused across workspaces with the division stored per connection (connection pool / backpressure deferred). Exact export automation runs as a local script; nightly sync + a Neno-GL migration path (stop sync, one-off reconciliation, then auto) designed.
First open-banking target customer is As Controla (no Swan account); provider selected is Montoya; onboarding calls targeted for 6-7 July with Joel executing while Matthew is away.

Onboarding is a fixed 6-step, self-reported checklist (connect Swan, connect external bank, view transactions, reconcile/respond to tasks, send first invoice, contact accountant); both the transactions drawer and the tasks/upload drawer expose all three bill-submission channels (WhatsApp, email forwarding, Vault).

open_questions.text: Accountant assignment for new workspaces: add to the accounting-engagements page (Yaroslav's) or hard-code for the initial five?
Open-banking prod-key gating requirements still being scoped (Montoya contract received 24 Jun).
When will the new transactions UI (by-date/by-status + open-tasks section) be ready? The full WhatsApp/email/Vault walkthrough depends on it.
Onboarding V2 deprecation date conflict: Swan email says 26 Jun, live docs say 30 Sep - Cecile to confirm.

next_steps.text: Write up the onboarding requirements before Matthew goes on leave.
Dima & Eugenia build out the onboarding steps next week, with a decision framework to resolve open questions independently.
Iterate on the onboarding prototype and the open-banking integration.
Fix the Transactions page visibility/permissions for open-banking customers (show once a bank is connected and transactions exist).
Test the magic-link onboarding flow end-to-end in a new workspace.
Test the full open-banking flow once Montoya prod keys arrive.

fef38f90-b82c-4c47-b377-6dcb9b1a3020.title: Enable accountants to complete VAT submissions via double-entry GL

notes.text: Full specification for this build lives in the native-GL knowledgebase (an Obsidian vault) on PR #1128: the user-observable outcomes in 'General Ledger - Truth Conditions' (TC-LEDGER-01..15), the system rules in 'General Ledger - Invariants' (INV-LEDGER-01..22), and the architecture narrative in the 'General Ledger' domain hub. In-scope-now items (TC-LEDGER-01..06 / INV-LEDGER-01..13) read 'Holds'; deferred future-build items (TC-LEDGER-07..15 / INV-LEDGER-14..22) read 'Proposed (deferred)'.

The knowledgebase is a behavioural 'digital twin': Truth Condition -> Invariant -> Scenario -> test -> code, traceable by id (TC-/INV-/SCN-LEDGER-NN). Tests cite the scenario id they prove; code cites the invariant id it satisfies. Reading order is top-down from the domain hub.
Agentic-ledger idea: when a backdated entry is posted, automatically detect which reports are affected and surface the delta.

fef38f90-b82c-4c47-b377-6dcb9b1a3020.exec_summary: Standing up Neno's own double-entry general ledger so accountants can file Q3 VAT for the first cohort from Neno's own data. The native GL (NEO-1361) is now implemented and covered by green tests on the build branch, gated behind an off-by-default feature flag pending a deliberate production go-live - superseding the earlier dual-write spike that mirrored Exact's codes. The design inverts the old model: Neno owns the chart of accounts and VAT codes, every accounting action emits an event that a reliable engine books into a balanced, VAT-aware journal entry with counterparty and VAT-code dimensions, and Exact becomes a removable downstream sync. Scope for this build is the VAT return, live/complete cash, append-only auditability and drill-down; period locking, opening balances, multi-currency, the full report set and XBRL filing are specified but deferred.
whats_changed: Native double-entry GL (NEO-1361) is built and green-tested on the build branch, behind an off-by-default feature flag; the 'ledger' event destination is now a supported, booked destination (PR #1128).

The work is now specified as a behavioural 'digital twin' knowledgebase (Truth Conditions -> Invariants -> Scenarios -> tests): TC-LEDGER-01..06 and INV-LEDGER-01..13 are in scope and now 'Hold'; TC-LEDGER-07..15 / INV-LEDGER-14..22 are specified but deferred.
Design supersedes the dual-write spike: Neno owns its chart of accounts and VAT codes; Exact is a removable downstream mapping the books never depend on.

VAT modelled as a first-class dimension - VAT code on both base and tax lines, treatment (domestic / reverse-charge / intra-EU / import / export) drives the posting shape; the VAT return is a code->box report, validated against a real filed Dutch return (Happy Team Apps Q1 2026).
Cash completeness confirmed: every bank transaction is booked on import (bank vs suspense) so the balance is live before reconciliation; reconciliation reclassifies out of suspense.
'Source of truth' deliberately reworded to 'source of truth for VAT filing' to avoid scope creep; euro-only for now with XBRL filing deferred.
Go-live now needs an opening-balance backfill or cutoff booking; year-end roll-forward (TC8) flagged CORE but still needs DP sign-off.

risks.text: Native GL build landed green-tested behind an off-by-default flag; risk has shifted from spike-code correctness to the production go-live cutover - opening-balance backfill/cutoff and Exact decoupling. (PR #1128)
Belgium gapless-ledger scope still unconfirmed - whether it constrains day-to-day ledger architecture or only closed-period exports/reporting.
DP's full reporting requirements list (potentially 100+ items) not yet compiled - could expand GL scope.

projects.name: Start writing to neno's double-entry GL
projects.summary: Native double-entry ledger now built and green-tested behind an off-by-default flag (NEO-1361): Neno-owned chart + VAT codes, event-driven balanced VAT-aware postings, append-only reversals, cash completeness, and the VAT return as a report. Exact decoupled to a removable downstream sync.
blockers: Production go-live cutover outstanding: opening-balance backfill or cutoff booking, plus Exact decoupling.
Year-end roll-forward (TC8) needs sign-off with DP before it is in scope.

decisions.text: Native GL (NEO-1361) is implemented and green-tested behind an off-by-default feature flag; Neno owns the chart and VAT codes and Exact becomes a removable downstream sync - superseding the dual-write spike.

Ledger architecture: every accounting action emits an event; a reliable engine books each into a balanced, VAT-aware journal entry with counterparty and VAT-code dimensions; entries reference their event + source; corrections are append-only contras.
VAT is a first-class dimension: VAT code on base and tax lines; treatment (domestic / reverse-charge / intra-EU / import / export) drives the posting shape; the return maps codes to boxes.
'Source of truth' reworded to 'source of truth for VAT filing' to avoid scope creep from external payment sources.
Book ledger entries when bank transactions import (not only bills/invoices) so the bank balance is knowable before reconciliation (cash completeness via suspense).
Euro-only for now; XBRL statutory filing deferred. Opening balances at go-live handled via a backfill or a cutoff booking.

VAT corrections after a period close are booked in the next period (dated day 1); under EUR 1,000 fold into the next return without a supplemental filing. Balance sheet needs three VAT lines: receivable, payable, corrections/suspense.
Closed periods lock writes by default; unlocking is approval-gated and only for material post-close accruals; insert-only with full audit log.
Insert-only ledger with reversals - no edits to journal entries; corrections reverse and rebook. Period closes define immutability. Monitoring over rollback. Euro-only, multi-currency deferred.

open_questions.text: Year-end roll-forward (close income/expense into retained earnings; clean new-year P&L + correct opening balance sheet) flagged CORE but still needs DP sign-off.
Belgium gapless-ledger requirement - does it constrain day-to-day ledger architecture or only closed-period exports/reporting?
Full reporting requirements list (potentially 100+ items) still being compiled by DP.
VAT filing period must be configurable per customer/country (monthly, quarterly, or bi-monthly).

next_steps.text: Finish and send the truth-conditions document.
Confirm TC8 / year-end roll-forward with DP.
Add XBRL to the Slack chat / roadmap.
Review spike code superseded; focus shifts to production go-live (opening balances + Exact decoupling).
Map the VAT export to Nextens for submission.

meetings.title: M&M
meetings.summary: Matthew/Mark working session on the native GL: scoped 'source of truth' to VAT filing, confirmed booking bank transactions on import, agreed supplemental-vs-break-the-glass handling, euro-only with XBRL later, and that opening balances need a backfill or cutoff booking.
decisions: Source of truth scoped to VAT filing
Book on import for cash completeness
Euro-only now, XBRL later
Opening balances via backfill or cutoff
open_questions: TC8 year-end roll-forward needs DP sign-off

meetings.title: Double-Entry Bookkeeping Open Questions
meetings.summary: Design session with DP and Mark that resolved the core ledger mechanics: VAT correction handling, closed-period locking, multi-currency FX, and the rate/territory VAT breakdown mapped to Nextens.
decisions: VAT corrections booked in the next period; <EUR 1,000 folds in
Closed periods lock; approval-gated insert-only with audit
Multi-currency: store txn-date rate, revalue at period-end
open_questions: Full reporting list pending from DP

ce07f00e-b392-40bd-85fb-2a4a831c9c80.title: Make neno the source of truth for all bookkeeping

notes.text: Feature set filed as Linear projects (Backlog) with DP-sourced requirements; requirements are updated on each project's summary directly for now, with the source noted.

ce07f00e-b392-40bd-85fb-2a4a831c9c80.exec_summary: The initiative is now filled out as a backlog: the full feature set has been filed as Linear projects (all Backlog), each with a summary and - where the 23 Jun DP session gave signal - initial requirements. End-to-end AR reconciliation is the one project further along (Planned, David). The DP session produced a detailed minimum reporting set (trial balance, full GL export, P&L, balance sheet, aged receivables, fixed-asset register, GL drill-down) plus richer requirements for related parties and depreciation/accruals, and restated the Q3 goal of 30 customers on neno feeding Exact. DP is still compiling a full reporting requirements list (100+ items) that will frame the scope.
whats_changed: Filed the feature set as Linear projects (Backlog) attached to the initiative, with DP-sourced requirements where available.

23 Jun DP session produced a minimum reporting set: trial balance, full GL export, P&L, balance sheet, aged receivables (30/60/90/120+ by customer), fixed-asset register with depreciation rates, and GL-account drill-down/export.
Related-parties requirement sharpened: every related-party transaction needs a counterparty tag/code and GL drill-down must show the related party per line.
Depreciation/accruals detail captured (asset-type grouping, fixed-asset register, annual review; accrual reverse-on-invoice workflow with an AI-assist opportunity).
Q3 goal restated: 30 customers running on neno for bookkeeping with neno as the single source of truth feeding Exact.
DP is compiling a full reporting requirements list (potentially 100+ items) to frame future design.

risks.text: Scope is broad and unsequenced; nothing is staffed yet beyond AR reconciliation being earmarked for David.
Full reporting requirements are not yet enumerated - scope could grow materially once DP's list lands.
~EUR 15K in outstanding receivables flagged with no active debtor management yet.

projects.name: End-to-end AR reconciliation
projects.summary: Match incoming payments to outbound invoices and close the open item in Exact; validated on seeded data first. ~EUR 15K outstanding receivables currently unmanaged - needs a debtor-management view.

projects.name: Financial reporting
projects.summary: Reporting on the ledger; minimum set = trial balance, full GL export, P&L, balance sheet, aged receivables (30/60/90/120+), fixed-asset register, GL drill-down; VAT breakdown by rate/territory to Nextens. Full list pending from DP.

projects.name: Credit note processing (AR & AP)
projects.summary: Process credit notes both ways so reconciliation and the ledger reflect reversals/refunds; still to be specced.

projects.name: Related-parties register
projects.summary: Register of related parties for intercompany payments; every related-party transaction carries a counterparty tag and GL drill-down shows the related party per line.

projects.name: Payroll (Numbrs integration)
projects.summary: Payroll via Numbrs; requirements not yet defined (not covered in the DP session).

projects.name: Third-party wallet transaction import (MVP)
projects.summary: Ingest third-party wallet/PSP transactions, Stripe first, so non-Swan banking customers feed transactions into neno.

projects.name: Depreciation & accruals
projects.summary: Depreciation by asset-type group with a fixed-asset register (annual review for now); accruals book expected expense and reverse on invoice arrival; post-close accruals via approval-gated inserts.

projects.name: Native team features & membership permissions
projects.summary: neno-native team/member management and permissions, independent of Swan.

projects.name: Customer project creation & management
projects.summary: Customer-defined projects for project-based cost/revenue tracking; scope TBD.

decisions.text: Three of the next-5 cohort run payroll that currently needs a manual monthly CSV import from Numbrs; payroll-integration viability for Q3 to be determined.
Aged-receivables analysis (by customer, 30/60/90/120+ day buckets) is a minimum AR reporting requirement.
Q3 goal restated: 30 customers running on neno for bookkeeping, with neno as the single source of truth feeding Exact.
Intercompany interest auto-accrual deprioritised as a nice-to-have (currently tracked in Excel).
Depreciation review runs in July and annually for now; year-end balance correctness is the key requirement (monthly journals possible in Exact).

open_questions.text: Payroll design not yet discussed - remains an open design area for a future session.
Full reporting requirements list still being compiled by DP.
Feature sequencing and ownership for the post-GL bookkeeping scope is not yet decided.
Ocean Ionics Espana: separate entity or a second bank account? Booking treatment (intercompany vs investment) depends on the answer.
VAT filing period flexibility (monthly/quarterly/bi-monthly) must be configurable per customer and country.

next_steps.text: Compile the full reporting requirements list.
Define and file the remaining projects after end-to-end AR reconciliation.

decisions: Minimum reporting set defined (trial balance, full GL export, P&L, balance sheet, aged receivables, fixed-asset register, GL drill-down)
Aged-receivables reporting (30/60/90/120+ buckets) confirmed as a minimum requirement
Intercompany interest auto-accrual deprioritised
